title: Dependency on multiple items

posts.author: Gil (Gil S T)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I’m trying to set up a project schedule to make automatic date adjustments to dependent items based on changes to precedent items.
Where an items has a single precedent item, using the dependency feature works well.
However, in most cases for me a dependent item’s timeline depends on multiple precedent items. i.e. I need the dependent item’s timeline to change in response to a change in the SUMMARY of the timeline of all the precedent items. The dependency for every item may be unique (i.e. some items may depend on 2 precedent items, others may depend on 8 precedent items).
My workaround for this has been to create a separate board replicating the dependent items, linking the replicas together and mirroring the timeline from the original board to the replica, then linking all the precedent activities from the original to each dependent’s replica on the other board (this makes the mirrored timeline column in the replica board summarise all the precedent items’ timelines). I then mirror the summarised timeline column from the replica board back to the original board so I can compare the “actual” timeline of the dependent item with the summarised timeline from the replica. Then I have to manually update the “actual” timeline to match the summarised timeline.
This is far from ideal because it’s very time consuming and confusing to manage, especially when an item is often dependent on various other items and also a precedent for multiple other items.
Is there a better way?

posts.author: QuadBlade (Christian)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does anyone have any reply to this?
Is it possible to create multiple dependencies for an item?

posts.author: omer.innoviz (Omer)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
Doesn’t work as needed, only for one depended item…

posts.author: fwoodley (Faye M Woodley)
posts.cookedHtml: I have this issue as well. I can’t use your workaround as I have too many boards to manage. I guess I’ll just add one dependent line. I’m also noticing when I have multiple dependencies, the dependencies set up after that multiple row don’t work either. You can only have one dependency or Monday doesn’t seem to work.

posts.author: odelegacy (Calvin Ode)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having the same issue. It would be great if multiple dependencies would work. I have tasks in the construction process that are dependent on multiple other tasks. If one is done early, I need the dependent task to adjust to the other task that hasn’t been completed yet.

posts.author: andrew.mikami (Andrew Mikami)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Has anyone found a good solution to this? I also have too many boards and dependencies to manually input the solution listed above.
It seems that Monday.com does allow for multiple dependencies for an item, but the automation is not useful my the project plan I am trying to make.
My project plan includes several items that are dependent on several different predecessors. I would like these dependent items to “monitor” the timelines of their predecessor items and start as soon as ALL of their predecessors are complete. Or at least keep the same finish-start relation to them as they started with. However it seems that the current Monday automation “Adjust the date of an item to reflect the changes made in the date of its dependency item” sums up the changes that were made to all of the predecessor items and pushes that to the dependent item.
For instance:
Predecessor 1 - shifts by 3 days
Predecessor 2 - shifts by 3 days
Predecessor 3 - shifts by 3 days
Dependent Item - shifts by 9 days

There is also another automation that “Ensure that the date of an item always starts after the date of its dependency item”. That one works well for pushing dates further into the future, but does nothing if a predecessor is completed ahead of schedule. This automation won’t ‘pull’ dependent items earlier to correspond with their predecessor being completed.

Is Monday working on, or does anyone know of a workaround to keep it as a simple Finish-to-Start dependency from many to one?

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @andrew.mikami! If you’re still running into these issues and limitations with dependencies, send us an email at support@monday.com and our automations squad will be able to figure out a workaround with you!
